summary: Frontend regression after react-hooks/exhaustive-deps cleanup (80 -> 0). Tested admin dashboard (all 7 tabs), NotificationBell, Marketplace, Demo user dashboard, and Notary dashboard. ZERO 'Maximum update depth exceeded' errors observed across all flows. No runaway network calls detected (all endpoints fired 1-2 times per mount, no >5 calls in 10s). The useMemo(headers) + mount-only effect-disable refactor is regression-clean.

design_issues.screen: Pre-existing console noise (NOT caused by hooks refactor)
issues: Hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option>/<select> (state/sort dropdowns in Marketplace or filters)
React key prop warning in Dashboard component render

action_items: (Optional, non-blocking) Fix pre-existing console warnings: <span> child of <select>/<option> hydration error, and missing key prop in Dashboard list render. Neither is related to the hooks-deps refactor.
critical_code_review_comments: Hooks refactor pattern is sound: making `headers` stable via useMemo([token]) correctly fixes the useCallback dep churn that would otherwise loop.

51 mount-only effect-disable comments are a pragmatic short-term fix; longer term these should be migrated to explicit dependency lists or extracted into custom hooks (e.g., useInitialFetch) to keep ESLint signal intact going forward.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hooks-deps refactor verified clean. Marketplace shows 4 notaries (John Notary CA $75, Notary Applicant c95c00b9 TX $0, Notary Applicant 3813cd5a TX $0, Test Notary Dashboard 5b675306 CA $0). Admin sees 37 users, 4 active notaries, 107 notarizations, $245 revenue, 3 pending apps. Demo user has 51 open requests, 14 sealed docs. Notary (John) has 47 pending in queue. Two pre-existing console warnings remain unrelated to hooks work.
